title: cerastium holosteoides subsp. triviale
intro: Cerastium holosteoides subsp. triviale, also known as a type of chickweed, is a low-growing, perennial plant native to North America. It has been used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, making it a potential remedy for various health issues. The plant's medicinal properties have been shown to have health benefits such as soothing skin irritations, reducing fever, and aiding in the treatment of wounds and infections. It can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat a range of ailments.
